Package io.fastpix.sdk.models.components

Class PatchResponseData

java.lang.Object
io.fastpix.sdk.models.components.PatchResponseData
public class PatchResponseData extends Object
PatchResponseData

Displays the result of the request.

    Optional<Boolean>
    closedCaptions()
    when provided true Enables closed captions for the livestream.
    Optional<OffsetDateTime>
    createdAt()
    It is the moment when the stream was created Time the media was generated, defined as a localDateTime (UTC Time).
    Optional<Boolean>
    enableDvrMode()
    Enables DVR (Digital Video Recorder) functionality for the live stream.
    Optional<Boolean>
    enableRecording()
    When set to true, the livestream will be recorded and stored for later viewing purposes.

    hashCode()
     
    Optional<Boolean>
    lowLatency()
    Enables low-latency streaming mode to reduce playback delay.
    Optional<Long>
    maxDuration()
    The maximum duration in seconds that a live stream can have before it ends the stream.
    Optional<String>
    maxResolution()
    Max resolution can be used to control the maximum resolution your media is encoded, stored, and streamed at.
    Optional<String>
    mediaPolicy()
    Determines whether the recorded stream must be publicly accessible or private in Live to VOD (Video on Demand).
    Optional<Map<String,String>>
    metadata()
    You can search for videos with specific key value pairs using metadata, when you tag a video in "key":"value"s pairs.
    Optional<List<PlaybackIdResponse>>
    playbackIds()
     
    Optional<Long>
    reconnectWindow()
    In case the software streaming the live, gets disrupted for any reason and gets disconnected from FastPix, the reconnect window specifies the time span FastPix will wait before ending the stream.
    Optional<SrtPlaybackResponse>
    srtPlaybackResponse()
    This object contains the livestream playback response details for SRT Protocol
    Optional<String>
    srtSecret()
    A secret used for securing the SRT stream.
    Optional<String>
    status()
    The current live stream status can be one of four values:Idle, Preparing, Active or Disabled.The Idle status signifies that there isn"t a broadcast in progress.The preparing status indicates that the stream is getting prepared.
    Optional<String>
    streamId()
    Upon creating a new live stream, FastPix assigns a unique identifier to the stream.
    Optional<String>
    streamKey()
    A unique stream key is generated for streaming, allowing the user to start streaming on any third-party platform using this key.

    FastPix allows for a to trial the live stream for free.

  • Constructor Details

    • PatchResponseData

      public PatchResponseData(@Nullable String streamId, @Nullable String streamKey, @Nullable String srtSecret, @Nullable Boolean trial, @Nullable String status, @Nullable String maxResolution, @Nullable Long maxDuration, @Nullable OffsetDateTime createdAt, @Nullable Long reconnectWindow, @Nullable Boolean enableRecording, @Nullable Boolean enableDvrMode, @Nullable String mediaPolicy, @Nullable Map<String,String> metadata, @Nullable Boolean lowLatency, @Nullable Boolean closedCaptions, @Nullable List<PlaybackIdResponse> playbackIds, @Nullable SrtPlaybackResponse srtPlaybackResponse)

    • PatchResponseData

      public PatchResponseData()

  • Method Details

    • streamId

      public Optional<String> streamId()
      Upon creating a new live stream, FastPix assigns a unique identifier to the stream.

    • streamKey

      public Optional<String> streamKey()
      A unique stream key is generated for streaming, allowing the user to start streaming on any third-party platform using this key.

    • srtSecret

      public Optional<String> srtSecret()
      A secret used for securing the SRT stream. This ensures that only authorized users can access the stream.

    • trial

      public Optional<Boolean> trial()
      FastPix allows for a to trial the live stream for free. The duration of trial streams is five minutes. After five minutes of activity, the trial stream is turned off, and the recorded asset is removed after a day.

    • status

      public Optional<String> status()
      The current live stream status can be one of four values:Idle, Preparing, Active or Disabled.The Idle status signifies that there isn"t a broadcast in progress.The preparing status indicates that the stream is getting prepared. while, the Active status indicates that a broadcast is currently in progress. The Disabled status means that no more RTMPS streams can be published.

    • maxResolution

      public Optional<String> maxResolution()
      Max resolution can be used to control the maximum resolution your media is encoded, stored, and streamed at.

    • maxDuration

      public Optional<Long> maxDuration()
      The maximum duration in seconds that a live stream can have before it ends the stream.

    • createdAt

      public Optional<OffsetDateTime> createdAt()
      It is the moment when the stream was created Time the media was generated, defined as a localDateTime (UTC Time).

    • reconnectWindow

      public Optional<Long> reconnectWindow()
      In case the software streaming the live, gets disrupted for any reason and gets disconnected from FastPix, the reconnect window specifies the time span FastPix will wait before ending the stream. Before starting the stream, you can set the reconnect window time which is up to 1800 seconds.

    • enableRecording

      public Optional<Boolean> enableRecording()
      When set to true, the livestream will be recorded and stored for later viewing purposes. If set to false, the livestream will not be recorded.

    • enableDvrMode

      public Optional<Boolean> enableDvrMode()
      Enables DVR (Digital Video Recorder) functionality for the live stream. When set to true, viewers can pause, rewind, and resume playback during the live broadcast. This allows time-shifted viewing of the stream while it is still ongoing.

    • mediaPolicy

      public Optional<String> mediaPolicy()
      Determines whether the recorded stream must be publicly accessible or private in Live to VOD (Video on Demand).

    • metadata

      public Optional<Map<String,String>> metadata()
      You can search for videos with specific key value pairs using metadata, when you tag a video in "key":"value"s pairs. Dynamic metadata allows you to define a key that allows any value pair. You can have maximum of 255 characters and upto 10 entries are allowed.

    • lowLatency

      public Optional<Boolean> lowLatency()
      Enables low-latency streaming mode to reduce playback delay.

    • closedCaptions

      public Optional<Boolean> closedCaptions()
      when provided true Enables closed captions for the livestream.

    • playbackIds

      public Optional<List<PlaybackIdResponse>> playbackIds()

    • srtPlaybackResponse

      public Optional<SrtPlaybackResponse> srtPlaybackResponse()
      This object contains the livestream playback response details for SRT Protocol

    • builder

      public static PatchResponseData.Builder builder()

    • withStreamId

      public PatchResponseData withStreamId(@Nullable String streamId)
      Upon creating a new live stream, FastPix assigns a unique identifier to the stream.

    • withStreamKey

      public PatchResponseData withStreamKey(@Nullable String streamKey)
      A unique stream key is generated for streaming, allowing the user to start streaming on any third-party platform using this key.

    • withSrtSecret

      public PatchResponseData withSrtSecret(@Nullable String srtSecret)
      A secret used for securing the SRT stream. This ensures that only authorized users can access the stream.

    • withTrial

      public PatchResponseData withTrial(@Nullable Boolean trial)
      FastPix allows for a to trial the live stream for free. The duration of trial streams is five minutes. After five minutes of activity, the trial stream is turned off, and the recorded asset is removed after a day.

    • withStatus

      public PatchResponseData withStatus(@Nullable String status)
      The current live stream status can be one of four values:Idle, Preparing, Active or Disabled.The Idle status signifies that there isn"t a broadcast in progress.The preparing status indicates that the stream is getting prepared. while, the Active status indicates that a broadcast is currently in progress. The Disabled status means that no more RTMPS streams can be published.

    • withMaxResolution

      public PatchResponseData withMaxResolution(@Nullable String maxResolution)
      Max resolution can be used to control the maximum resolution your media is encoded, stored, and streamed at.

    • withMaxDuration

      public PatchResponseData withMaxDuration(@Nullable Long maxDuration)
      The maximum duration in seconds that a live stream can have before it ends the stream.

    • withCreatedAt

      public PatchResponseData withCreatedAt(@Nullable OffsetDateTime createdAt)
      It is the moment when the stream was created Time the media was generated, defined as a localDateTime (UTC Time).

    • withReconnectWindow

      public PatchResponseData withReconnectWindow(@Nullable Long reconnectWindow)
      In case the software streaming the live, gets disrupted for any reason and gets disconnected from FastPix, the reconnect window specifies the time span FastPix will wait before ending the stream. Before starting the stream, you can set the reconnect window time which is up to 1800 seconds.

    • withEnableRecording

      public PatchResponseData withEnableRecording(@Nullable Boolean enableRecording)
      When set to true, the livestream will be recorded and stored for later viewing purposes. If set to false, the livestream will not be recorded.

    • withEnableDvrMode

      public PatchResponseData withEnableDvrMode(@Nullable Boolean enableDvrMode)
      Enables DVR (Digital Video Recorder) functionality for the live stream. When set to true, viewers can pause, rewind, and resume playback during the live broadcast. This allows time-shifted viewing of the stream while it is still ongoing.

    • withMediaPolicy

      public PatchResponseData withMediaPolicy(@Nullable String mediaPolicy)
      Determines whether the recorded stream must be publicly accessible or private in Live to VOD (Video on Demand).

    • withMetadata

      public PatchResponseData withMetadata(@Nullable Map<String,String> metadata)
      You can search for videos with specific key value pairs using metadata, when you tag a video in "key":"value"s pairs. Dynamic metadata allows you to define a key that allows any value pair. You can have maximum of 255 characters and upto 10 entries are allowed.

    • withLowLatency

      public PatchResponseData withLowLatency(@Nullable Boolean lowLatency)
      Enables low-latency streaming mode to reduce playback delay.

    • withClosedCaptions

      public PatchResponseData withClosedCaptions(@Nullable Boolean closedCaptions)
      when provided true Enables closed captions for the livestream.

    • withPlaybackIds

      public PatchResponseData withPlaybackIds(@Nullable List<PlaybackIdResponse> playbackIds)

    • withSrtPlaybackResponse

      public PatchResponseData withSrtPlaybackResponse(@Nullable SrtPlaybackResponse srtPlaybackResponse)
      This object contains the livestream playback response details for SRT Protocol
